steps.title: Sketch the main body (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch the mailbox body — a tall rectangular box with a rounded top. Think of it as a rectangle with a half-circle sitting on it. The box is roughly as wide as it is tall.

steps.title: Add the mail slot (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch the slot on the front face: a narrow horizontal rectangle about a third of the way up the body, centered left-to-right. This is the most recognizable feature.

steps.title: Sketch the pedestal (pencil)
steps.description: Add the narrow column below the box and the square footing at the very bottom. The pedestal is thinner than the box — it shouldn't look like it could fall over, but it should look slender.

steps.title: Refine the rounded top (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a vertical center line from the arch peak down through the body and pedestal — the mailbox is symmetrical around this axis. Check that the arch looks balanced on each side. Erase and redraw either side if it's off.

steps.title: Switch to ink and draw the arch
steps.description: Switch to ink. Ink the arch at the top first — a smooth left-side stroke, then a matching right-side stroke, meeting at the peak. Starting with the arch anchors everything else.

steps.title: Ink the body, slot, and pedestal (ink)
steps.description: Ink the straight sides of the box body down from the arch, the front face lines, and the slot. Then ink the pedestal column and its base. Add a small circular badge or emblem on the front face — a simple ring with a dot — to suggest the postal service marking.
